title: Versioned SQL Migrations, Backfills, Compatibility, and Recovery · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds compatible TypeScript service migration chain from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of compatible TypeScript service migration chain while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Keep immutable ordered migrations, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, status inspection, and generated artifact review under source control.
Choose transactional and nontransactional boundaries for locks, concurrent indexes, data changes, and failure recovery.
Stage additive schema, bounded backfill, compatibility reads and writes, validation, cutover, and cleanup across deployed versions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Migration Ledger
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6524 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, resumable backfill, and recovery; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, entity, date, identity, or runtime-validation case and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep immutable ordered migrations, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, status inspection, and generated artifact review under source control.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Migration Ledger.

Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: feed-ts-migration-test-matrix-1etzyb" that uses case 6146 to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, resumable backfill, and recovery.
